crazy:weather-derivative-momentum.title: Weather Derivative Momentum
crazy:weather-derivative-momentum.thesis: Certain commodities and sectors are highly sensitive to short-term regional weather anomalies. By tracking daily deviations in temperature and precipitation from historical averages in key agricultural and energy-producing regions, we can generate momentum signals predicting commodity price moves. This approach exploits the lag between weather impacts and market price adjustments.
data_sources: NOAA Climate Data Online (CDO)
USDA Crop Progress Reports
EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report
crazy:weather-derivative-momentum.signal_logic: Calculate daily temperature and precipitation anomalies for major commodity-producing regions (e.g., Midwest US for corn, Texas for oil). If a 3-day moving average anomaly exceeds +2 standard deviations for temperature or precipitation, generate a momentum signal favoring bullish exposure to that commodity. Conversely, anomalies below -2 standard deviations generate bearish signals.
crazy:weather-derivative-momentum.entry_exit: Enter long positions when anomaly > +2 SD sustained for 3 days; exit when anomaly returns within ±1 SD. Enter short positions when anomaly < -2 SD sustained for 3 days; exit when anomaly returns within ±1 SD.
crazy:weather-derivative-momentum.position_sizing: Allocate 5-10% of portfolio per position, scaled by the magnitude of the anomaly (e.g., 2 SD = 5%, 3 SD = 10%). Limit total exposure to 20% to manage risk.
crazy:weather-derivative-momentum.risks: Weather anomalies may not always translate to price moves due to inventory buffers or market expectations. False signals can occur during seasonal transitions. Sudden policy changes or supply shocks unrelated to weather can invalidate signals.
crazy:weather-derivative-momentum.implementation_notes: 1) Obtain historical daily temperature and precipitation data from NOAA for target regions. 2) Calculate anomalies vs. 10-year daily averages. 3) Compute moving averages and standard deviations. 4) Map anomalies to commodities based on regional relevance. 5) Fetch commodity price data for signal validation. 6) Backtest signal logic on historical data.
crazy:weather-derivative-momentum.public_summary: Certain commodities and sectors are highly sensitive to short-term regional weather anomalies. By tracking daily deviations in temperature and precipitation from historical averages in key agricultural and energy-producing regions, we can generate momentum signals predicting commodity price moves. This approach exploits the lag between weather impacts and market price adjustments.

crazy:coffee-shop-wifi-usage.title: Coffee Shop WiFi Usage
crazy:coffee-shop-wifi-usage.thesis: The relative number of people using WiFi at popular coffee shop chains can signal changes in foot traffic and consumer spending in those areas.
data_sources: Foursquare location data
Public WiFi usage statistics
crazy:coffee-shop-wifi-usage.signal_logic: Monitor the relative WiFi usage at major coffee shop chains in different neighborhoods. Look for changes in usage that deviate from seasonal norms or the average across all locations.
crazy:coffee-shop-wifi-usage.entry_exit: Buy when WiFi usage at a location increases significantly above the chain average. Sell when usage returns to normal levels.
crazy:coffee-shop-wifi-usage.position_sizing: Allocate a small percentage of the portfolio (e.g., 2-5%) to each coffee shop location trade, diversifying across multiple chains and neighborhoods.
crazy:coffee-shop-wifi-usage.risks: Foot traffic and WiFi usage can be affected by factors beyond just consumer spending, like weather, local events, or network outages. Liquidity may be low for smaller coffee shop chains.
crazy:coffee-shop-wifi-usage.implementation_notes: Collect Foursquare location data and public WiFi usage statistics. Analyze trends in relative usage across locations to generate buy/sell signals. Monitor for anomalies and adjust thresholds accordingly.
crazy:coffee-shop-wifi-usage.public_summary: The relative number of people using WiFi at popular coffee shop chains can signal changes in foot traffic and consumer spending in those areas.

crazy:news-sentiment-rotation-fade-trade.title: News Sentiment Rotation Fade Trade
crazy:news-sentiment-rotation-fade-trade.thesis: When combined recession + earnings miss mentions spike to 90th percentile, sentiment capitulation is near; market contrarian reversal opportunity follows within 3–5 days. Extreme negative sentiment clustering often marks washout phase; consumer discretionary rebounds as capitulation exhausts.

crazy:rental-car-shortage-volatility-spi.title: Rental Car Shortage Volatility Spike
crazy:rental-car-shortage-volatility-spi.thesis: Spikes in rental car availability/pricing searches correlate with tight vehicle supply, rising leisure travel demand, or fleet disruptions—both inflationary and demand signals. crazy:seismic-activity-construction-halt.title: Seismic Activity Construction Halt Signal
crazy:seismic-activity-construction-halt.thesis: Clusters of moderate earthquakes (M4.5+) in major construction zones halt projects, disrupt supply chains, and create insurance/liability spikes in affected regions. Building materials demand falls during seismic event construction halts; project delays inflate materials inventory and reduce near-term demand.
data_sources: USGS earthquake activity (magnitude ≥4.5) by region through earthquake_activity adapter
crazy:seismic-activity-construction-halt.signal_logic: When 2+ earthquakes M≥4.5 occur within same tectonic region within 48 hours
crazy:seismic-activity-construction-halt.entry_exit: Entry: When 2+ earthquakes M≥4.5 occur within same tectonic region within 48 hours Exit: After 7 trading days or when seismic activity subsides (<1 event per week in region)
crazy:seismic-activity-construction-halt.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:seismic-activity-construction-halt.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:seismic-activity-construction-halt.implementation_notes: Use USGS earthquake activity (magnitude ≥4.5) by region through earthquake_activity adapter via api (daily).
crazy:seismic-activity-construction-halt.high_action_metadata: - Expected Fire Rate: weekly - Historical Backfill: True - Minimum History Months: 24 - Adapter Status: existing - Trigger Sensitivity: high - Why It Should Fire Soon: Earthquake clusters are non-uniform but frequent; California/Japan/Chile produce 2–4 M4.5+ clusters per month on average.

crazy:shipping-container-squeeze-play.title: Shipping Container Squeeze Play
crazy:shipping-container-squeeze-play.thesis: Elevated container backlogs in major Asian ports signal supply chain tightness and rising freight costs, inflating input prices for manufacturing and retail. Manufacturing sector faces margin compression from elevated logistics costs; industrials are most exposed to freight cost pass-through friction.
data_sources: Port of Shanghai weekly container export volumes and backlog through port_container_volume adapter (public shipping indices)
crazy:shipping-container-squeeze-play.signal_logic: When Shanghai port weekly export backlog exceeds 90th percentile of 52-week rolling history
crazy:shipping-container-squeeze-play.entry_exit: Entry: When Shanghai port weekly export backlog exceeds 90th percentile of 52-week rolling history Exit: After 10 trading days or when backlog normalizes below 70th percentile

crazy:shipping-container-squeeze-play.implementation_notes: Use Port of Shanghai weekly container export volumes and backlog through port_container_volume adapter (public shipping indices) via api (weekly).

crazy:temperature-shock-energy-demand-fl.title: Temperature Shock Energy Demand Flip
crazy:temperature-shock-energy-demand-fl.thesis: When temperature deviation from 30-year normal exceeds ±2 standard deviations for 3+ consecutive days, it forces emergency energy demand swings and utility margin pressure. Utilities face volume-price mismatches during extreme weather; margin compression and regulatory backlash pressure utility sector equity returns.
data_sources: Open-Meteo daily average temperature anomalies for US and Europe through weather_series adapter
crazy:temperature-shock-energy-demand-fl.signal_logic: When daily US temperature anomaly ≥2σ from historical median AND persists for ≥3 consecutive days
crazy:temperature-shock-energy-demand-fl.entry_exit: Entry: When daily US temperature anomaly ≥2σ from historical median AND persists for ≥3 consecutive days Exit: After 5 trading days or when temperature normalizes to within 1σ
crazy:temperature-shock-energy-demand-fl.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.

crazy:unemployment-claim-anticipation-he.title: Unemployment Claim Anticipation Hedge
crazy:unemployment-claim-anticipation-he.thesis: When initial jobless claims spike above 2-week moving average by >10%, it signals near-term labor market stress and often precedes equity volatility. Claims spikes pressure financial sector credit quality and loan loss provisions; weakness in financial stocks often leads broader selloffs.
data_sources: FRED series ICSA (Initial Claims, weekly) through fred_series adapter
crazy:unemployment-claim-anticipation-he.signal_logic: When ICSA exceeds its 2-week SMA by ≥10% and closes above prior week's high
crazy:unemployment-claim-anticipation-he.entry_exit: Entry: When ICSA exceeds its 2-week SMA by ≥10% and closes above prior week's high Exit: After 5 trading days or when ICSA reverts below 1.05× 2-week SMA
crazy:unemployment-claim-anticipation-he.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:unemployment-claim-anticipation-he.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:unemployment-claim-anticipation-he.implementation_notes: Use FRED series ICSA (Initial Claims, weekly) through fred_series adapter via api (weekly).
crazy:unemployment-claim-anticipation-he.high_action_metadata: - Expected Fire Rate: weekly - Historical Backfill: True - Minimum History Months: 24 - Adapter Status: existing - Trigger Sensitivity: high - Why It Should Fire Soon: Weekly claims data ensures signal recurrence; seasonal layoffs and economic cycles produce 2–3 spike events per month on average.
crazy:unemployment-claim-anticipation-he.public_summary: When initial jobless claims spike above 2-week moving average by >10%, it signals near-term labor market stress and often precedes equity volatility. crazy:airline-load-factor-compression-fr.title: Airline Load Factor Compression From Weak Business Travel
crazy:airline-load-factor-compression-fr.thesis: Load factors fall when business travel weakens (corporate cost-cutting, recession fear). Falling load factors below 80% compress airline pricing power, which then ripples into hospitality and leisure discretionary weakness. Load factor compression signals demand destruction in high-margin business travel; airlines cut capacity and pricing, hotels face lower occupancy.
data_sources: BTS airline load factor (weekly average via bts_airline_load_factor adapter) plus XLY (consumer discretionary) weekly close via price_only adapter
crazy:airline-load-factor-compression-fr.signal_logic: If BTS weekly load factor drops >2 percentage points in a single week AND load factor < 81% AND XLY closes below 50-day SMA
crazy:airline-load-factor-compression-fr.entry_exit: Entry: If BTS weekly load factor drops >2 percentage points in a single week AND load factor < 81% AND XLY closes below 50-day SMA Exit: After 14 calendar days OR if load factor recovers above 82% for 2 consecutive weeks
crazy:airline-load-factor-compression-fr.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:airline-load-factor-compression-fr.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:airline-load-factor-compression-fr.implementation_notes: Use BTS airline load factor (weekly average via bts_airline_load_factor adapter) plus XLY (consumer discretionary) weekly close via price_only adapter via api (weekly).
crazy:airline-load-factor-compression-fr.high_action_metadata: - Expected Fire Rate: monthly - Historical Backfill: True - Minimum History Months: 12 - Adapter Status: existing - Trigger Sensitivity: high - Why It Should Fire Soon: Seasonal business travel cycles and quarterly earnings cuts trigger load factor drops every 6–8 weeks.

crazy:energy-intensive-manufacturing-piv.title: Energy-intensive Manufacturing Pivot Away From Peak-hours Eia Demand Spike Fade
crazy:energy-intensive-manufacturing-piv.thesis: When peak-hour electricity demand spikes >3% above rolling 20-day average on same day, it signals industrial production surge. Industrials rally, utilities face margin pressure. Fade occurs as demand normalization follows within 2–3 weeks. Peak electricity demand spikes are a real-time read on factory utilization; rallies in industrial output drive equipment/machinery demand.
data_sources: EIA daily electricity demand (peak hour demand via eia_electricity adapter) plus XLI (industrials) and XLU (utilities) daily closes via price_only adapter
crazy:energy-intensive-manufacturing-piv.signal_logic: If EIA peak-hour demand > 20-day MA × 1.03 AND XLI closes > 5-day SMA AND XLI volume > 10-day MA
crazy:energy-intensive-manufacturing-piv.entry_exit: Entry: If EIA peak-hour demand > 20-day MA × 1.03 AND XLI closes > 5-day SMA AND XLI volume > 10-day MA Exit: After 10 trading days OR if EIA demand falls below 20-day MA for 3 consecutive days
crazy:energy-intensive-manufacturing-piv.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:energy-intensive-manufacturing-piv.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:energy-intensive-manufacturing-piv.implementation_notes: Use EIA daily electricity demand (peak hour demand via eia_electricity adapter) plus XLI (industrials) and XLU (utilities) daily closes via price_only adapter via api (daily).

crazy:freight-rail-carload-plunge-from-m.title: Freight Rail Carload Plunge From Manufacturing Slowdown
crazy:freight-rail-carload-plunge-from-m.thesis: Carload volumes fall sharply when manufacturing activity weakens. A >5% drop in weekly carloads vs 4-week MA signals demand destruction; industrials and materials underperform within 2 weeks. Rail carload data is a real-time proxy for industrial production; sharp declines precede earnings misses and guidance cuts.
data_sources: FRED series RAILFRTCG (railroad freight carloads, weekly) via fred_series adapter, plus XLI (industrials) weekly close via price_only adapter
crazy:freight-rail-carload-plunge-from-m.signal_logic: If RAILFRTCG weekly < 4-week MA × 0.95 AND XLI closes below 20-day SMA AND XLI RSI(14) < 50
crazy:freight-rail-carload-plunge-from-m.entry_exit: Entry: If RAILFRTCG weekly < 4-week MA × 0.95 AND XLI closes below 20-day SMA AND XLI RSI(14) < 50 Exit: After 10 trading days OR if RAILFRTCG recovers to 4-week MA for 2 consecutive weeks
crazy:freight-rail-carload-plunge-from-m.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:freight-rail-carload-plunge-from-m.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:freight-rail-carload-plunge-from-m.implementation_notes: Use FRED series RAILFRTCG (railroad freight carloads, weekly) via fred_series adapter, plus XLI (industrials) weekly close via price_only adapter via api (weekly).

crazy:healthcare-cost-panic-from-medical.title: Healthcare Cost Panic From Medical Billing Scandal News Surge
crazy:healthcare-cost-panic-from-medical.thesis: Spikes in news coverage of medical billing errors, surprise hospital bills, or insurance denials trigger consumer anxiety. Healthcare stocks dip on regulatory scrutiny fear, then recover as overshooting subsides. News surges on billing scandals create temporary fear of margin compression and regulatory action, causing tactical healthcare sector weakness.

crazy:healthcare-cost-panic-from-medical.entry_exit: Entry: If weekly RSS article count from healthcare billing topics exceeds 200% of 4-week MA AND XLV closes < 20-day SMA AND article count rises >50% week-over-week Exit: After 7 calendar days OR if article count falls below 120% of 4-week MA
crazy:healthcare-cost-panic-from-medical.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:healthcare-cost-panic-from-medical.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.

crazy:mortgage-rate-shock-yield-curve-st.title: Mortgage Rate Shock Yield-curve Steepening Hedge
crazy:mortgage-rate-shock-yield-curve-st.thesis: When 2-10 year spread widens >25 bps in a single week after being inverted or flat, bond market is pricing in rate-cut expectations. Utilities and REITs rally on lower discount rates. Steeper curves and falling long rates compress the required return on stable utility cash flows, driving re-rating upward.
data_sources: FRED series DGS10 (10-year yield) and DGS2 (2-year yield) daily via fred_series adapter, plus XLU (utilities ETF) daily close via price_only adapter
crazy:mortgage-rate-shock-yield-curve-st.signal_logic: If (DGS10 - DGS2) increases by >25 bps over 5 trading days AND XLU closes above 20-day SMA AND 10-year yield drops >10 bps from 5-day high
crazy:mortgage-rate-shock-yield-curve-st.entry_exit: Entry: If (DGS10 - DGS2) increases by >25 bps over 5 trading days AND XLU closes above 20-day SMA AND 10-year yield drops >10 bps from 5-day high Exit: After 10 trading days OR if spread compresses <15 bps from entry level
crazy:mortgage-rate-shock-yield-curve-st.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:mortgage-rate-shock-yield-curve-st.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:mortgage-rate-shock-yield-curve-st.implementation_notes: Use FRED series DGS10 (10-year yield) and DGS2 (2-year yield) daily via fred_series adapter, plus XLU (utilities ETF) daily close via price_only adapter via api (daily).

crazy:natural-gas-futures-volatility-spi.title: Natural Gas Futures Volatility Spike From Weather Extremes
crazy:natural-gas-futures-volatility-spi.thesis: Extreme temperature swings (heating/cooling demand shocks) drive natural gas price spikes. When daily temp anomaly exceeds ±2 standard deviations AND UNG volume surges 40% above 20-day average, supply anxiety triggers bullish energy positioning. Natural gas is a primary energy input; supply stress immediately lifts integrated energy and downstream utility hedges.
data_sources: Open-Meteo daily temperature anomalies (5-day rolling avg deviation from 30-year baseline) plus Yahoo Finance UNG daily close via price_only adapter
crazy:natural-gas-futures-volatility-spi.signal_logic: When 5-day rolling temp anomaly exceeds ±2σ AND UNG volume > 1.4× 20-day MA AND UNG closes up >2% on the day
crazy:natural-gas-futures-volatility-spi.entry_exit: Entry: When 5-day rolling temp anomaly exceeds ±2σ AND UNG volume > 1.4× 20-day MA AND UNG closes up >2% on the day Exit: After 7 calendar days OR when temp anomaly returns to within ±1σ for 3 consecutive days
crazy:natural-gas-futures-volatility-spi.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:natural-gas-futures-volatility-spi.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:natural-gas-futures-volatility-spi.implementation_notes: Use Open-Meteo daily temperature anomalies (5-day rolling avg deviation from 30-year baseline) plus Yahoo Finance UNG daily close via price_only adapter via api (daily).

crazy:seismic-activity-construction-insu.title: Seismic Activity Construction Insurance Premium Spike
crazy:seismic-activity-construction-insu.thesis: Clusters of >3.0 magnitude earthquakes in major metro areas spike construction insurance demand and delay project starts. Materials demand softens as contractors postpone work, but insurance/reinsurance stocks see margin improvement. Earthquake swarms delay construction spending and postpone materials purchases; materials producers face near-term demand destruction.

crazy:seismic-activity-construction-insu.signal_logic: If 3+ earthquakes >3.0 magnitude occur within a 7-day window in CA/WA/OK AND XLB closes < 10-day SMA AND XLB shows negative momentum (close < open for 2+ of last 3 days)
crazy:seismic-activity-construction-insu.entry_exit: Entry: If 3+ earthquakes >3.0 magnitude occur within a 7-day window in CA/WA/OK AND XLB closes < 10-day SMA AND XLB shows negative momentum (close < open for 2+ of last 3 days) Exit: After 14 calendar days OR if no magnitude >3.0 events occur for 5 consecutive days
crazy:seismic-activity-construction-insu.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:seismic-activity-construction-insu.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:seismic-activity-construction-insu.implementation_notes: Use USGS earthquake activity daily API (magnitude >3.0 events in construction-heavy regions: CA, WA, OK) via earthquake_activity adapter, plus XLB (materials/construction) weekly close via price_only adapter via api (daily).

crazy:bank-stress-index-volatility-fear-.title: Bank Stress Index Volatility Fear Crush Reversal
crazy:bank-stress-index-volatility-fear-.thesis: VIX spikes >25 while XLF underperforms SPY by >2%, then reverses sharply as Fed rate-cut expectations emerge, driving financials outperformance. Financial sector rallies when fear subsides and rate expectations stabilize in their favor.
data_sources: Price_only adapter for financial sector ETF XLF and VIX via Yahoo Finance; daily data
crazy:bank-stress-index-volatility-fear-.signal_logic: If VIX > 25 and XLF lags SPY by >2% over 3 days, then VIX falls >5 points in 1 day, long XLF
crazy:bank-stress-index-volatility-fear-.entry_exit: Entry: If VIX > 25 and XLF lags SPY by >2% over 3 days, then VIX falls >5 points in 1 day, long XLF Exit: Exit after 8 trading days or if XLF underperforms again
crazy:bank-stress-index-volatility-fear-.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:bank-stress-index-volatility-fear-.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:bank-stress-index-volatility-fear-.implementation_notes: Use Price_only adapter for financial sector ETF XLF and VIX via Yahoo Finance; daily data via api (daily).
crazy:bank-stress-index-volatility-fear-.high_action_metadata: - Expected Fire Rate: monthly - Historical Backfill: True - Minimum History Months: 12 - Adapter Status: existing - Trigger Sensitivity: high - Why It Should Fire Soon: Daily VIX and ETF data; fear/relief cycles occur 4–6 times per quarter in response to Fed announcements and earnings surprises.
crazy:bank-stress-index-volatility-fear-.public_summary: VIX spikes >25 while XLF underperforms SPY by >2%, then reverses sharply as Fed rate-cut expectations emerge, driving financials outperformance. Financial sector rallies when fear subsides and rate expectations stabilize in their favor.

crazy:commercial-real-estate-distress-fl.title: Commercial Real Estate Distress Floor Collapse Signal

data_sources: FRED series MORTGAGE30US (30-year mortgage rate) via fred_series adapter
crazy:commercial-real-estate-distress-fl.signal_logic: If weekly mortgage rate drop > 50 bps from prior week close, short XLRE
crazy:commercial-real-estate-distress-fl.entry_exit: Entry: If weekly mortgage rate drop > 50 bps from prior week close, short XLRE Exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if rates stabilize/rise for 2 consecutive weeks

crazy:commercial-real-estate-distress-fl.public_summary: 30-year mortgage rates drop >0.5% in a single week, signaling flight-to-safety demand, which precedes weakness in REITs and property valuations. crazy:temperature-extremes-power-demand-.title: Temperature Extremes Power Demand Spike Utility Boost

crazy:jobless-claims-momentum-reversal-f.thesis: Week-over-week drops in jobless claims of 8%+ often signal labor market stabilization and consumer confidence inflection, driving consumer discretionary and financials outperformance. Declining jobless claims reduce economic recession risk and support consumer spending and credit confidence.

crazy:jobless-claims-momentum-reversal-f.entry_exit: Entry: If weekly jobless claims drop 8% or more week-over-week AND claims fall below 4-week moving average Exit: After 8 trading days or if claims spike above prior week's level

crazy:natural-gas-volatility-crush-rever.title: Natural Gas Volatility Crush Reversal
crazy:natural-gas-volatility-crush-rever.thesis: When natural gas volatility drops below 30th percentile and price stabilizes above 50-day MA, it signals completion of selloff and impending recovery momentum, supporting energy sector rotations. Low volatility combined with price support often precedes natural gas recovery rallies and improved energy company cash flows.

crazy:airline-fuel-surcharge-announcemen.title: Airline Fuel Surcharge Announcement Spike

crazy:airline-load-factor-collapse-rebou.title: Airline Load Factor Collapse Rebound Trade
crazy:airline-load-factor-collapse-rebou.thesis: When airline load factors drop 3+ percentage points week-over-week (signaling demand shock), they historically bounce back 2+ points within 2 weeks as airlines cut capacity and demand stabilizes. Load factor collapse and recovery cycle creates near-term airline margin tailwinds and travel discretionary strength.
data_sources: BTS airline load factor weekly data via html_table scrape

crazy:fred-commercial-paper-stress-widen.title: Fred Commercial Paper Stress Widening Bearish Fade
crazy:fred-commercial-paper-stress-widen.thesis: Widening spreads between AA-rated and Prime commercial paper signal corporate credit stress and rising default fear, leading to flight-to-safety and sector rotation away from cyclicals. Commercial paper stress is a leading indicator of credit-sensitive equity weakness, especially consumer discretionary.
data_sources: FRED series AA-rated vs. Prime commercial paper spread (MMNRNJ vs MMNRJD)
crazy:fred-commercial-paper-stress-widen.signal_logic: If CP spread widens by more than 15 bps in one week and remains elevated, short XLY.
crazy:fred-commercial-paper-stress-widen.entry_exit: Entry: If CP spread widens by more than 15 bps in one week and remains elevated, short XLY. Exit: After 12 trading days or if spread tightens by 10 bps.

crazy:fred-yield-curve-inversion-reversi.title: Fred Yield Curve Inversion Reversion Momentum Snap
crazy:fred-yield-curve-inversion-reversi.thesis: When the yield curve inverts (2yr > 10yr) for 3+ weeks then suddenly re-steepens by 15+ bps in one week, it signals a capitulation reversal and flight-to-risk rally. Curve un-inversion after sustained inversion triggers risk-on rotation; consumer discretionary leads the rebound.
data_sources: FRED 10-year vs 2-year yield spread (T10Y2Y)
crazy:fred-yield-curve-inversion-reversi.signal_logic: If curve has been inverted for 20+ days and spreads 20+ bps steeper in one week, long XLY.
crazy:fred-yield-curve-inversion-reversi.entry_exit: Entry: If curve has been inverted for 20+ days and spreads 20+ bps steeper in one week, long XLY. Exit: After 7 trading days or if curve re-inverts.
crazy:fred-yield-curve-inversion-reversi.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:fred-yield-curve-inversion-reversi.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:fred-yield-curve-inversion-reversi.implementation_notes: Use FRED 10-year vs 2-year yield spread (T10Y2Y) via api (daily).

crazy:freight-rail-carload-collapse-logi.title: Freight Rail Carload Collapse Logistics Sector Fade
crazy:freight-rail-carload-collapse-logi.thesis: When weekly rail carloads drop 5%+ vs prior year AND are below 52-week median, it signals demand destruction across commodity and manufacturing chains; XLI reverses lower. Rail carloads are a coincident indicator of industrial production and logistics demand; collapses signal recession or inventory reduction.

crazy:healthcare-cost-sentiment-rss-news.title: Healthcare Cost Sentiment Rss News Volume Spike Volatility Play
crazy:healthcare-cost-sentiment-rss-news.thesis: When daily RSS news mentions of healthcare costs, drug prices, or insurance premiums spike 60%+ above rolling 30-day average, it signals public outcry and policy risk; XLV volatility spikes 20%+. Sudden healthcare cost sentiment surges trigger regulatory risk fears and sector repricing; short-term volatility crushes buyer confidence.

crazy:oil-price-momentum-reversal-energy.title: Oil Price Momentum Reversal Energy Sector Bounce
crazy:oil-price-momentum-reversal-energy.thesis: When crude oil closes down 3+ days straight but bounces 2%+ on the 4th day and XLE lags, mean reversion pressure builds; XLE outperforms over next 5 days. Oil bounce signals reversal of supply shock fears; energy equities repricing higher on macro stabilization.

crazy:unemployment-claims-surprise-volat.title: Unemployment Claims Surprise Volatility Pop Trade
crazy:unemployment-claims-surprise-volat.thesis: When weekly jobless claims exceed prior week by 10k+ AND exceed 52-week average by 15%+, it signals labor market deterioration; XLF and XLI reverse hard over next 5-10 days as recession fears spike and fade. Jobless claims spikes trigger cyclical sector selloffs; financials and industrials are hit first due to credit cycle fears.

crazy:airline-load-factor-stress-pricing.title: Airline Load Factor Stress Pricing Power Collapse
crazy:airline-load-factor-stress-pricing.thesis: When weekly airline load factor (% seats filled) falls >2.5% from prior 4-week rolling average while available seat miles remain flat, pricing power collapses. Carriers cut fares and margins compress. Load factor collapse signals consumer travel demand softness and forces airlines to discount capacity, eroding margins and reducing equity valuations.
data_sources: BTS airline load factor and available seat miles via FRED/BTS adapter, weekly series
crazy:airline-load-factor-stress-pricing.signal_logic: Weekly load factor >2.5% below 4-week rolling average AND seat miles YoY flat to +1%
crazy:airline-load-factor-stress-pricing.entry_exit: Entry: Weekly load factor >2.5% below 4-week rolling average AND seat miles YoY flat to +1% Exit: Load factor rebounds above 4-week rolling mean or 14 trading days elapsed
crazy:airline-load-factor-stress-pricing.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:airline-load-factor-stress-pricing.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:airline-load-factor-stress-pricing.implementation_notes: Use BTS airline load factor and available seat miles via FRED/BTS adapter, weekly series via api (weekly).

crazy:bank-deposit-outflow-fear-inversio.title: Bank Deposit Outflow Fear Inversion Signal
crazy:bank-deposit-outflow-fear-inversio.thesis: When weekly bank deposit flows turn negative (outflows exceed inflows) for 2 consecutive weeks AND high-yield savings rates spike >0.5% above prior month, retail depositors flee traditional banks. Bank funding stress accelerates. Deposit outflows force banks to raise rates, compress NIM, and rely on wholesale funding. Equity valuations contract on net interest margin compression.
data_sources: FRED series on bank deposits (BMNRNJ) and high-yield savings rate (MMNRNJ) weekly; XLF ETF daily price via price_only adapter
crazy:bank-deposit-outflow-fear-inversio.signal_logic: Weekly deposit growth <0% for 2 consecutive weeks AND HY savings rate spike >0.5% MoM
crazy:bank-deposit-outflow-fear-inversio.entry_exit: Entry: Weekly deposit growth <0% for 2 consecutive weeks AND HY savings rate spike >0.5% MoM Exit: Deposit growth returns >0.5% or 15 trading days elapsed
crazy:bank-deposit-outflow-fear-inversio.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:bank-deposit-outflow-fear-inversio.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:bank-deposit-outflow-fear-inversio.implementation_notes: Use FRED series on bank deposits (BMNRNJ) and high-yield savings rate (MMNRNJ) weekly; XLF ETF daily price via price_only adapter via api (weekly).

crazy:commodity-input-cost-shock-mining-.title: Commodity Input Cost Shock Mining Sector Margin Crunch
crazy:commodity-input-cost-shock-mining-.thesis: When FRED commodity index (broad basket) spikes >3% in a single day while XLB stays flat or declines, input costs surge faster than mining/materials producer pricing. Margin squeeze imminent. Materials producers cannot immediately pass through input cost shocks; operating margins compress until prices normalize or they cut production.
data_sources: FRED commodity price indices (energy, metals, agricultural) daily series; XLB ETF daily price via price_only adapter
crazy:commodity-input-cost-shock-mining-.signal_logic: Commodity index +3% day AND XLB +0.5% or lower same day
crazy:commodity-input-cost-shock-mining-.entry_exit: Entry: Commodity index +3% day AND XLB +0.5% or lower same day Exit: XLB closes >1.5% above entry close or 8 trading days elapsed
crazy:commodity-input-cost-shock-mining-.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:commodity-input-cost-shock-mining-.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:commodity-input-cost-shock-mining-.implementation_notes: Use FRED commodity price indices (energy, metals, agricultural) daily series; XLB ETF daily price via price_only adapter via api (daily).
crazy:commodity-input-cost-shock-mining-.high_action_metadata: - Expected Fire Rate: weekly - Historical Backfill: True - Minimum History Months: 24 - Adapter Status: existing - Trigger Sensitivity: high - Why It Should Fire Soon: Daily commodity shocks >3% occur 2–3 times per month. Divergence with materials ETF pricing happens 4–6 times annually.

crazy:ev-charger-installation-slowdown-s.title: Ev Charger Installation Slowdown Sentiment Fade
crazy:ev-charger-installation-slowdown-s.thesis: When weekly EV charger installation growth (count delta) falls >15% below its 12-week rolling average, it signals EV infrastructure investment cooling. Sentiment on clean energy capex fades. EV charger slowdown signals weakening clean tech capex and consumer EV adoption confidence, pressuring semiconductor and software exposure in energy transition.
data_sources: OpenChargeMap API monthly charger count growth; cross-reference with FRED vehicle sales series
crazy:ev-charger-installation-slowdown-s.signal_logic: Weekly charger net adds fall >15% below 12-week rolling average; close below prior week close
crazy:ev-charger-installation-slowdown-s.entry_exit: Entry: Weekly charger net adds fall >15% below 12-week rolling average; close below prior week close Exit: Weekly adds rebound above 10-week rolling average or 12 trading days elapsed
crazy:ev-charger-installation-slowdown-s.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:ev-charger-installation-slowdown-s.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:ev-charger-installation-slowdown-s.implementation_notes: Use OpenChargeMap API monthly charger count growth; cross-reference with FRED vehicle sales series via api (weekly).
crazy:ev-charger-installation-slowdown-s.high_action_metadata: - Expected Fire Rate: monthly - Historical Backfill: True - Minimum History Months: 24 - Adapter Status: existing - Trigger Sensitivity: high - Why It Should Fire Soon: EV charging growth is lumpy; seasonality and policy cycles cause >15% swings 3–4 times per year. Signal matures in 2–3 weeks.

crazy:freight-volume-collapse-on-weak-tr.title: Freight Volume Collapse On Weak Trucking Orders
crazy:freight-volume-collapse-on-weak-tr.thesis: When weekly truck tonnage declines >3% week-over-week for 2 consecutive weeks, logistics providers face utilization collapse and pricing pressure. Industrial demand weakens ahead of broader slowdown. Trucking tonnage is a lead indicator of industrial production and capex demand; sustained declines signal margin compression across industrials and transportation.
data_sources: FRED series TOTALSA (total truck tonnage and carloads) via API
crazy:freight-volume-collapse-on-weak-tr.signal_logic: Weekly tonnage MoM decline >3% for 2 consecutive weeks; cumulative 2-week decline >6%
crazy:freight-volume-collapse-on-weak-tr.entry_exit: Entry: Weekly tonnage MoM decline >3% for 2 consecutive weeks; cumulative 2-week decline >6% Exit: Tonnage rebounds >1% week-over-week or 15 trading days elapsed
crazy:freight-volume-collapse-on-weak-tr.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:freight-volume-collapse-on-weak-tr.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:freight-volume-collapse-on-weak-tr.implementation_notes: Use FRED series TOTALSA (total truck tonnage and carloads) via API via api (weekly).
crazy:freight-volume-collapse-on-weak-tr.high_action_metadata: - Expected Fire Rate: weekly - Historical Backfill: True - Minimum History Months: 36 - Adapter Status: existing - Trigger Sensitivity: high - Why It Should Fire Soon: Freight tonnage is volatile; 3% weekly swings occur 4–6 times annually. Signal fires regularly outside holiday periods.

crazy:real-estate-cap-rate-compression-y.title: Real Estate Cap Rate Compression Yield Chase Unwind
crazy:real-estate-cap-rate-compression-y.thesis: When 10-year mortgage rates fall >15 bps in a week but XLRE underperforms SPY by >1.5%, yield-chasing capital rotates away from real estate. Sentiment on cap rate compression fades. Rate cuts typically boost REIT valuations; when they don't, it signals investor skepticism about fundamental demand or dividend sustainability.
data_sources: FRED commercial real estate cap rate series (MORTGAGE30US, TERMCBCCALLNS) daily; XLRE ETF daily price via price_only adapter
crazy:real-estate-cap-rate-compression-y.signal_logic: 10-year mortgage rate falls >15 bps weekly AND XLRE underperforms SPY by >1.5% over same period
crazy:real-estate-cap-rate-compression-y.entry_exit: Entry: 10-year mortgage rate falls >15 bps weekly AND XLRE underperforms SPY by >1.5% over same period Exit: XLRE outperforms SPY >1% or 12 trading days elapsed
crazy:real-estate-cap-rate-compression-y.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:real-estate-cap-rate-compression-y.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:real-estate-cap-rate-compression-y.implementation_notes: Use FRED commercial real estate cap rate series (MORTGAGE30US, TERMCBCCALLNS) daily; XLRE ETF daily price via price_only adapter via api (daily).

crazy:recession-whisper-google-trends-un.title: Recession Whisper Google Trends Unemployment Search Surge
crazy:recession-whisper-google-trends-un.thesis: When combined search interest in unemployment + layoff terms spikes >40% above 52-week baseline in a single week, consumer anxiety peaks. Historical lead to risk-off rotation. Unemployment search spikes precede consumer spending weakness; discretionary retailers face demand headwinds 2–4 weeks later.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search volume for terms: 'unemployment benefits', 'job loss', 'layoffs near me'
crazy:recession-whisper-google-trends-un.signal_logic: Combined normalized search score >140 (baseline 100) and >40% above 52-week mean
crazy:recession-whisper-google-trends-un.entry_exit: Entry: Combined normalized search score >140 (baseline 100) and >40% above 52-week mean Exit: Score falls below 120 or 20 trading days elapsed
crazy:recession-whisper-google-trends-un.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:recession-whisper-google-trends-un.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:recession-whisper-google-trends-un.implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search volume for terms: 'unemployment benefits', 'job loss', 'layoffs near me' via api (weekly).

crazy:utility-grid-strain-heat-wave-dema.title: Utility Grid Strain Heat Wave Demand Spike
crazy:utility-grid-strain-heat-wave-dema.thesis: When regional max temperatures exceed 95°F for 3+ consecutive days AND EIA electricity demand rises above its 30-day rolling average by >2%, grid strain intensifies and utilities face margin compression. Utilities see margin compression during demand spikes without proportional rate relief; operating costs rise faster than revenue.
data_sources: Open-Meteo daily temperature anomalies and FRED electricity load series (API EIA_ELECTRICITY_DEMAND)
crazy:utility-grid-strain-heat-wave-dema.signal_logic: Max temp >95°F for 3 consecutive days AND EIA demand >102% of 30-day rolling mean
crazy:utility-grid-strain-heat-wave-dema.entry_exit: Entry: Max temp >95°F for 3 consecutive days AND EIA demand >102% of 30-day rolling mean Exit: Temperature falls below 90°F or EIA demand normalizes below 100.5% of rolling mean, or after 10 trading days
crazy:utility-grid-strain-heat-wave-dema.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:utility-grid-strain-heat-wave-dema.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:utility-grid-strain-heat-wave-dema.implementation_notes: Use Open-Meteo daily temperature anomalies and FRED electricity load series (API EIA_ELECTRICITY_DEMAND) via api (daily).
crazy:utility-grid-strain-heat-wave-dema.high_action_metadata: - Expected Fire Rate: weekly - Historical Backfill: True - Minimum History Months: 24 - Adapter Status: existing - Trigger Sensitivity: high - Why It Should Fire Soon: Summer heat waves occur reliably; electricity demand data updates daily. Signal fires multiple times annually during June–September.
crazy:utility-grid-strain-heat-wave-dema.public_summary: When regional max temperatures exceed 95°F for 3+ consecutive days AND EIA electricity demand rises above its 30-day rolling average by >2%, grid strain intensifies and utilities face margin compression. crazy:wage-growth-demand-surge-fast-food.title: Wage Growth Demand Surge Fast Food Google Trends
crazy:wage-growth-demand-surge-fast-food.thesis: When combined search interest spikes >35% above 52-week baseline, it signals labor tightness in low-wage sectors. crazy:weather-driven-agricultural-future.title: Weather-driven Agricultural Futures Shock Consumer Food Inflation
crazy:weather-driven-agricultural-future.thesis: When grain belt precipitation falls >50% below normal for 14+ consecutive days AND FRED agricultural commodity indices spike >2.5% in a single week, food input costs surge. Consumer staples pricing power tested. Food inflation pressures staples consumers; if staples raise prices too much, volume declines. If they absorb costs, margins compress. Either way, equity returns suffer.
data_sources: Open-Meteo precipitation and temperature daily data for US grain belt; cross-reference FRED agricultural commodity prices weekly
crazy:weather-driven-agricultural-future.signal_logic: Grain belt precip <50% of 30-year normal for 14+ days AND ag commodity index +2.5% weekly
crazy:weather-driven-agricultural-future.entry_exit: Entry: Grain belt precip <50% of 30-year normal for 14+ days AND ag commodity index +2.5% weekly Exit: Precip rebounds to >70% normal or commodity index declines >1% or 20 trading days elapsed
crazy:weather-driven-agricultural-future.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:weather-driven-agricultural-future.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:weather-driven-agricultural-future.implementation_notes: Use Open-Meteo precipitation and temperature daily data for US grain belt; cross-reference FRED agricultural commodity prices weekly via api (daily).

crazy:airline-load-factor-collapse-beari.title: Airline Load Factor Collapse Bearish Play
crazy:airline-load-factor-collapse-beari.thesis: When load factors (seats sold / seats available) drop 3+ percentage points week-over-week, airlines cut capacity and raise fares; near-term bookings weaken, signaling demand deterioration. Airline load factor weakness signals recession-like travel demand pullback affecting leisure and hospitality.
data_sources: BTS weekly airline load factor via bts_airline_load_factor adapter
crazy:airline-load-factor-collapse-beari.signal_logic: If weekly BTS load factor falls 3+ points vs. prior week and falls below its 8-week rolling average
crazy:airline-load-factor-collapse-beari.entry_exit: Entry: If weekly BTS load factor falls 3+ points vs. prior week and falls below its 8-week rolling average Exit: After 4 weeks or when load factor recovers 2+ points from entry level
crazy:airline-load-factor-collapse-beari.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:airline-load-factor-collapse-beari.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:airline-load-factor-collapse-beari.implementation_notes: Use BTS weekly airline load factor via bts_airline_load_factor adapter via api (weekly).
crazy:airline-load-factor-collapse-beari.high_action_metadata: - Expected Fire Rate: monthly - Historical Backfill: True - Minimum History Months: 12 - Adapter Status: existing - Trigger Sensitivity: medium - Why It Should Fire Soon: Load factor swings of 3+ points occur most months; seasonal dips create reliable signal fire.

crazy:earthquake-swarm-near-oil-refinery.title: Earthquake Swarm Near Oil Refinery Cluster Signal
crazy:earthquake-swarm-near-oil-refinery.thesis: When a cluster of 5+ small earthquakes (magnitude 3.0-4.5) occurs within 50 km of a major US refinery zone in a single week, it signals potential refinery operational stress or community concern; downstream energy prices rise and energy stocks underperform. Seismic activity near refineries creates operational risk perception and potential supply disruption fears.
data_sources: USGS earthquake activity daily data via earthquake_activity adapter, filtered for Texas Gulf Coast and Southern California refinery zones
crazy:earthquake-swarm-near-oil-refinery.signal_logic: If 5+ earthquakes magnitude 3.0-4.5 occur within 50 km of major refinery cluster in a single week, AND XLE volume exceeds 20-day average by 25%
crazy:earthquake-swarm-near-oil-refinery.entry_exit: Entry: If 5+ earthquakes magnitude 3.0-4.5 occur within 50 km of major refinery cluster in a single week, AND XLE volume exceeds 20-day average by 25% Exit: After 4 weeks or when earthquake activity returns to background rates (1-2 events per week in zone)
crazy:earthquake-swarm-near-oil-refinery.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:earthquake-swarm-near-oil-refinery.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:earthquake-swarm-near-oil-refinery.implementation_notes: Use USGS earthquake activity daily data via earthquake_activity adapter, filtered for Texas Gulf Coast and Southern California refinery zones via api (daily).

crazy:ev-charger-installation-accelerati.title: Ev Charger Installation Acceleration Play
crazy:ev-charger-installation-accelerati.thesis: When the weekly rate of new EV charger installations accelerates (week-over-week growth jumps 20%+), it signals municipal/private infrastructure spending surge; this often precedes renewable energy demand and tech equipment procurement. EV charger deployment requires semiconductors, software, and networking gear; acceleration signals tech capex cycle.

crazy:financial-sector-volatility-crush-.title: Financial Sector Volatility Crush Trade
crazy:financial-sector-volatility-crush-.thesis: When VIX drops 3+ points in a single day while the financial sector (XLF) simultaneously gaps down, it signals a washout in financial stocks despite falling macro volatility—often creating a mean-reversion bounce within days. Financial stocks are oversold when VIX falls but sector falls harder; mean reversion favors the sector.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance daily prices for XLF and VIX via price_only adapter
crazy:financial-sector-volatility-crush-.signal_logic: If VIX falls 3+ points day-over-day AND XLF underperforms SPY by 2%+ on the same day AND XLF closes near its lows
crazy:financial-sector-volatility-crush-.entry_exit: Entry: If VIX falls 3+ points day-over-day AND XLF underperforms SPY by 2%+ on the same day AND XLF closes near its lows Exit: After 5 trading days or once XLF closes 1.5%+ above entry
crazy:financial-sector-volatility-crush-.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:financial-sector-volatility-crush-.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:financial-sector-volatility-crush-.implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance daily prices for XLF and VIX via price_only adapter via api (daily).
crazy:financial-sector-volatility-crush-.high_action_metadata: - Expected Fire Rate: weekly - Historical Backfill: True - Minimum History Months: 12 - Adapter Status: existing - Trigger Sensitivity: high - Why It Should Fire Soon: VIX daily moves of 3+ points occur several times per month; financial sector gaps down on these days frequently.
crazy:financial-sector-volatility-crush-.public_summary: When VIX drops 3+ points in a single day while the financial sector (XLF) simultaneously gaps down, it signals a washout in financial stocks despite falling macro volatility—often creating a mean-reversion bounce within days. Financial stocks are oversold when VIX falls but sector falls harder; mean reversion favors the sector.

crazy:freight-rail-carload-collapse-sign.title: Freight Rail Carload Collapse Signal
crazy:freight-rail-carload-collapse-sign.thesis: When weekly carloads (cars loaded with freight) drop 8%+ vs. year-ago week, it signals industrial production weakness; this often precedes equity weakness in materials and industrials. Carload collapse is a leading indicator of manufacturing and construction slowdown.
data_sources: Association of American Railroads weekly carload data via fred_series adapter (AAR feeds FRED)
crazy:freight-rail-carload-collapse-sign.signal_logic: If weekly carloads fall 8%+ year-over-year AND are below their 13-week rolling average
crazy:freight-rail-carload-collapse-sign.entry_exit: Entry: If weekly carloads fall 8%+ year-over-year AND are below their 13-week rolling average Exit: After 6 weeks or when carloads stabilize above the 13-week average
crazy:freight-rail-carload-collapse-sign.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:freight-rail-carload-collapse-sign.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:freight-rail-carload-collapse-sign.implementation_notes: Use Association of American Railroads weekly carload data via fred_series adapter (AAR feeds FRED) via api (weekly).

crazy:gold-volatility-vol-of-vol-spike.title: Gold Volatility Vol-of-vol Spike
crazy:gold-volatility-vol-of-vol-spike.thesis: When the intraday volatility of gold prices begins spiking (vol-of-vol), it signals macro uncertainty; this often precedes a reallocation into defensive commodities. Tech and growth equities underperform when gold volatility rises, signaling flight-to-safety behavior.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance daily prices for GLD and USO via price_only adapter
crazy:gold-volatility-vol-of-vol-spike.signal_logic: If 5-day rolling standard deviation of GLD daily returns exceeds 2x the 60-day rolling mean, AND GLD closes above its 20-day MA
crazy:gold-volatility-vol-of-vol-spike.entry_exit: Entry: If 5-day rolling standard deviation of GLD daily returns exceeds 2x the 60-day rolling mean, AND GLD closes above its 20-day MA Exit: After 7 trading days or when vol-of-vol reverts to mean (5-day std dev falls below 1.5x 60-day mean)
crazy:gold-volatility-vol-of-vol-spike.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:gold-volatility-vol-of-vol-spike.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:gold-volatility-vol-of-vol-spike.implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance daily prices for GLD and USO via price_only adapter via api (daily).

crazy:mortgage-refinance-wave-google-tre.title: Mortgage Refinance Wave Google Trends Surge
crazy:mortgage-refinance-wave-google-tre.thesis: When mortgage refinance searches spike, it signals households are actively managing debt stress; subsequent weeks show either housing demand relief (XLY bullish) or wallet tightening (XLP bullish). Successful refinances free up monthly cash flow for discretionary consumer spending.

crazy:small-cap-growth-momentum-rotation.title: Small-cap Growth Momentum Rotation
crazy:small-cap-growth-momentum-rotation.thesis: When IWM (small-cap) 5-day momentum outpaces QQQ (large-cap growth) by 2% or more, it signals a rotation into lower-liquidity names—often driven by retail attention shifts visible in option flow and social media chatter. Small-cap rotation favors consumer discretionary and beaten-down cyclical names.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance daily prices for IWM (Russell 2000) and QQQ via price_only adapter
crazy:small-cap-growth-momentum-rotation.signal_logic: If IWM 5-day return minus QQQ 5-day return exceeds +2% AND IWM volume is 15%+ above 20-day average
crazy:small-cap-growth-momentum-rotation.entry_exit: Entry: If IWM 5-day return minus QQQ 5-day return exceeds +2% AND IWM volume is 15%+ above 20-day average Exit: After 6 trading days or if relative momentum reverses (IWM underperforms by 1% or more)
crazy:small-cap-growth-momentum-rotation.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:small-cap-growth-momentum-rotation.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:small-cap-growth-momentum-rotation.implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance daily prices for IWM (Russell 2000) and QQQ via price_only adapter via api (daily).

crazy:utility-demand-weather-whipsaw.title: Utility Demand Weather Whipsaw
crazy:utility-demand-weather-whipsaw.thesis: Sudden swings in seasonal temperature create rapid demand shocks for electricity and natural gas, forcing utility operators to adjust generation and buying patterns within hours. Utilities rally on unexpected demand spikes that increase margin and utilization rates.

crazy:utility-demand-weather-whipsaw.entry_exit: Entry: If 7-day rolling average temperature change exceeds ±8°F from prior week and XLU volume spikes 30% above 20-day average Exit: After 8 trading days or when temperature stabilizes within 5°F of seasonal norm
crazy:utility-demand-weather-whipsaw.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:utility-demand-weather-whipsaw.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.

crazy:airline-load-factor-decline-stress.title: Airline Load Factor Decline Stress Test
crazy:airline-load-factor-decline-stress.thesis: Weekly average airline load factor drops 3%+ from prior week, signaling sudden demand weakness or capacity glut in air travel. Airline load factor collapse signals consumer travel pullback and discretionary spending recession.
data_sources: BTS (Bureau of Transportation Statistics) airline load factor and capacity reports through bts_airline_load_factor adapter
crazy:airline-load-factor-decline-stress.signal_logic: If latest week load factor is 3%+ below prior week and below 52-week median
crazy:airline-load-factor-decline-stress.entry_exit: Entry: If latest week load factor is 3%+ below prior week and below 52-week median Exit: After 7 trading days or once load factor recovers to within 1% of prior-week average
crazy:airline-load-factor-decline-stress.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:airline-load-factor-decline-stress.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:airline-load-factor-decline-stress.implementation_notes: Use BTS (Bureau of Transportation Statistics) airline load factor and capacity reports through bts_airline_load_factor adapter via api (weekly).
crazy:airline-load-factor-decline-stress.high_action_metadata: - Expected Fire Rate: weekly - Historical Backfill: True - Minimum History Months: 24 - Adapter Status: existing - Trigger Sensitivity: high - Why It Should Fire Soon: Load factors are volatile week-to-week due to seasonality; 3% drops occur 1-2 times monthly on average.
crazy:airline-load-factor-decline-stress.public_summary: Weekly average airline load factor drops 3%+ from prior week, signaling sudden demand weakness or capacity glut in air travel. Airline load factor collapse signals consumer travel pullback and discretionary spending recession.

crazy:bank-stock-volatility-spike-compre.title: Bank Stock Volatility Spike Compression Release
crazy:bank-stock-volatility-spike-compre.thesis: 30-day rolling volatility on XLF drops to lowest level in 90 days, followed by close above upper Bollinger Band, signaling compression breakout. Volatility compression in financial sector often precedes relief rallies when risk appetite returns or rate environment stabilizes.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance daily prices and intraday volatility for XLF (Financials ETF) through price_only adapter
crazy:bank-stock-volatility-spike-compre.signal_logic: If XLF 30-day volatility is lowest in 90 days AND XLF closes above 20-day Bollinger upper band
crazy:bank-stock-volatility-spike-compre.entry_exit: Entry: If XLF 30-day volatility is lowest in 90 days AND XLF closes above 20-day Bollinger upper band Exit: After 6 trading days or once XLF volume drops below 20-day average
crazy:bank-stock-volatility-spike-compre.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:bank-stock-volatility-spike-compre.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:bank-stock-volatility-spike-compre.implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance daily prices and intraday volatility for XLF (Financials ETF) through price_only adapter via api (daily).

crazy:bank-stock-volatility-spike-compre.public_summary: 30-day rolling volatility on XLF drops to lowest level in 90 days, followed by close above upper Bollinger Band, signaling compression breakout. crazy:earthquake-swarm-insurance-reprici.title: Earthquake Swarm Insurance Repricing
crazy:earthquake-swarm-insurance-reprici.thesis: Cluster of 5+ magnitude 3.0+ earthquakes in a single week in a populated region triggers investor concern about insurable losses and re-insurance repricing. Seismic activity clusters raise loss reserve expectations and insurance premium pressure, impacting financial sector profitability.

crazy:electricity-demand-spike-weather-a.title: Electricity Demand Spike Weather Anomaly
crazy:electricity-demand-spike-weather-a.thesis: Daily electricity demand exceeds 30-day average by 15% while temperature is 10+ degrees above seasonal normal, signaling heat stress and utility margin compression. Extreme demand spikes during heat waves reduce utility margins, strain grid capacity, and increase regulatory scrutiny.
data_sources: EIA electricity demand (total US, daily) through eia_electricity adapter combined with Open-Meteo daily temperature through weather_series adapter
crazy:electricity-demand-spike-weather-a.signal_logic: If daily electricity demand is 15%+ above 30-day rolling average and daily temp is 10+ degrees above normal for region
crazy:electricity-demand-spike-weather-a.entry_exit: Entry: If daily electricity demand is 15%+ above 30-day rolling average and daily temp is 10+ degrees above normal for region Exit: After 5 trading days or when daily demand returns to within 5% of 30-day average
crazy:electricity-demand-spike-weather-a.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:electricity-demand-spike-weather-a.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.

Extreme demand spikes during heat waves reduce utility margins, strain grid capacity, and increase regulatory scrutiny.
data_sources: EIA electricity demand (total US, daily) through eia_electricity adapter combined with Open-Meteo daily temperature through weather_series adapter
electricity-demand-spike-weather-anomaly.signal_logic: If daily electricity demand is 15%+ above 30-day rolling average and daily temp is 10+ degrees above normal for region
electricity-demand-spike-weather-anomaly.entry_exit: Entry: If daily electricity demand is 15%+ above 30-day rolling average and daily temp is 10+ degrees above normal for region Exit: After 5 trading days or when daily demand returns to within 5% of 30-day average
electricity-demand-spike-weather-anomaly.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
electricity-demand-spike-weather-anomaly.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
electricity-demand-spike-weather-anomaly.implementation_notes: Use EIA electricity demand (total US, daily) through eia_electricity adapter combined with Open-Meteo daily temperature through weather_series adapter via api (daily).
electricity-demand-spike-weather-anomaly.high_action_metadata: - Expected Fire Rate: daily - Historical Backfill: True - Minimum History Months: 24 - Adapter Status: existing - Trigger Sensitivity: high - Why It Should Fire Soon: Heat waves and demand spikes occur 4-6 times in summer months; threshold fires multiple times per June-September.
electricity-demand-spike-weather-anomaly.public_summary: Daily electricity demand exceeds 30-day average by 15% while temperature is 10+ degrees above seasonal normal, signaling heat stress and utility margin compression. Extreme demand spikes during heat waves reduce utility margins, strain grid capacity, and increase regulatory scrutiny.

crazy:ev-charger-expansion-frenzy.title: Ev Charger Expansion Frenzy
crazy:ev-charger-expansion-frenzy.thesis: Weekly net growth in public EV chargers exceeds 2% of prior week's total, signaling acceleration in infrastructure deployment and capital spending. Charger buildout drives industrial equipment orders, electrical infrastructure upgrades, and construction activity.
data_sources: OpenChargeMap API daily charger count by region through openchargemap adapter
crazy:ev-charger-expansion-frenzy.signal_logic: If week-over-week net charger additions exceed 2% and 7-day price momentum on XLI is positive
crazy:ev-charger-expansion-frenzy.entry_exit: Entry: If week-over-week net charger additions exceed 2% and 7-day price momentum on XLI is positive Exit: After 10 trading days or if weekly charger growth drops below 0.5%
crazy:ev-charger-expansion-frenzy.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:ev-charger-expansion-frenzy.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:ev-charger-expansion-frenzy.implementation_notes: Use OpenChargeMap API daily charger count by region through openchargemap adapter via api (daily).
crazy:ev-charger-expansion-frenzy.high_action_metadata: - Expected Fire Rate: weekly - Historical Backfill: True - Minimum History Months: 12 - Adapter Status: existing - Trigger Sensitivity: high - Why It Should Fire Soon: Charger deployment is steady and accelerates seasonally in spring; 2% weekly growth threshold fires multiple times per quarter.
crazy:ev-charger-expansion-frenzy.public_summary: Weekly net growth in public EV chargers exceeds 2% of prior week's total, signaling acceleration in infrastructure deployment and capital spending. Charger buildout drives industrial equipment orders, electrical infrastructure upgrades, and construction activity.

crazy:fred-m2-money-supply-growth-oscill.title: Fred M2 Money Supply Growth Oscillation Trade
crazy:fred-m2-money-supply-growth-oscill.thesis: Weekly M2 growth rate crosses from positive to negative or vice versa; reversal signals shift in Fed liquidity stance or unexpected monetary tightening. Technology and growth stocks are most sensitive to liquidity reversals; sudden M2 contraction triggers defensive rebalancing into cash.
data_sources: FRED M2 Money Supply (weekly) series WIMFSL through fred_series adapter
crazy:fred-m2-money-supply-growth-oscill.signal_logic: If 4-week M2 growth rate changes sign (pos→neg or neg→pos) and 10-year yield spreads widen by 20+ bps
crazy:fred-m2-money-supply-growth-oscill.entry_exit: Entry: If 4-week M2 growth rate changes sign (pos→neg or neg→pos) and 10-year yield spreads widen by 20+ bps Exit: After 8 trading days or once M2 growth rate stabilizes in one direction for 3 consecutive weeks
crazy:fred-m2-money-supply-growth-oscill.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:fred-m2-money-supply-growth-oscill.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:fred-m2-money-supply-growth-oscill.implementation_notes: Use FRED M2 Money Supply (weekly) series WIMFSL through fred_series adapter via api (weekly).
crazy:fred-m2-money-supply-growth-oscill.high_action_metadata: - Expected Fire Rate: weekly - Historical Backfill: True - Minimum History Months: 24 - Adapter Status: existing - Trigger Sensitivity: medium - Why It Should Fire Soon: M2 growth oscillates every 4-8 weeks; sign reversals occur 6-8 times per year, especially during Fed policy transitions.
crazy:fred-m2-money-supply-growth-oscill.public_summary: Weekly M2 growth rate crosses from positive to negative or vice versa; reversal signals shift in Fed liquidity stance or unexpected monetary tightening. Technology and growth stocks are most sensitive to liquidity reversals; sudden M2 contraction triggers defensive rebalancing into cash.

crazy:google-trends-surge-in-used-car-pr.title: Google Trends Surge In Used Car Price Searches
crazy:google-trends-surge-in-used-car-pr.thesis: Spike in searches for used car pricing signals consumer anxiety about vehicle affordability and resale values, often preceding used car market weakness. Used car price stress ripples into auto retail, financing, and discretionary consumer spending on vehicles and related services.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'used car prices' and 'car price drop' through google_trends adapter
crazy:google-trends-surge-in-used-car-pr.signal_logic: If Google Trends search volume for 'used car prices' rises 40% week-over-week and stays above 60-week median
crazy:google-trends-surge-in-used-car-pr.entry_exit: Entry: If Google Trends search volume for 'used car prices' rises 40% week-over-week and stays above 60-week median Exit: After 8 trading days or if search volume normalizes to 50-week average
crazy:google-trends-surge-in-used-car-pr.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:google-trends-surge-in-used-car-pr.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:google-trends-surge-in-used-car-pr.implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search volume for 'used car prices' and 'car price drop' through google_trends adapter via api (weekly).
crazy:google-trends-surge-in-used-car-pr.high_action_metadata: - Expected Fire Rate: weekly - Historical Backfill: True - Minimum History Months: 12 - Adapter Status: existing - Trigger Sensitivity: high - Why It Should Fire Soon: Consumer automotive searches spike monthly during rate hikes, supply shocks, and seasonal transitions; threshold is loose enough to trigger 3-4 times per quarter.
crazy:google-trends-surge-in-used-car-pr.public_summary: Spike in searches for used car pricing signals consumer anxiety about vehicle affordability and resale values, often preceding used car market weakness. Used car price stress ripples into auto retail, financing, and discretionary consumer spending on vehicles and related services.

crazy:healthcare-cost-shock-google-trend.title: Healthcare Cost Shock Google Trends Spike

crazy:rail-carload-contraction-signal.title: Rail Carload Contraction Signal
crazy:rail-carload-contraction-signal.thesis: Weekly total carloads drop 8% or more from prior-year equivalent week, signaling sharp freight demand collapse and economic slowdown. Rail carload data is a leading indicator of industrial production, construction input, and commodity demand.
data_sources: Association of American Railroads weekly carload reports through html_table adapter scraping AAR public tables
crazy:rail-carload-contraction-signal.signal_logic: If latest week carloads are 8%+ below prior-year same week and 4-week average is negative
crazy:rail-carload-contraction-signal.entry_exit: Entry: If latest week carloads are 8%+ below prior-year same week and 4-week average is negative Exit: After 10 trading days or when year-over-year carloads return to +2%
crazy:rail-carload-contraction-signal.position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
crazy:rail-carload-contraction-signal.risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
crazy:rail-carload-contraction-signal.implementation_notes: Use Association of American Railroads weekly carload reports through html_table adapter scraping AAR public tables via scrape (weekly).
crazy:rail-carload-contraction-signal.high_action_metadata: - Expected Fire Rate: weekly - Historical Backfill: True - Minimum History Months: 36 - Adapter Status: existing - Trigger Sensitivity: medium - Why It Should Fire Soon: Carload volatility is seasonal; 8% YoY declines occur 2-4 times per year during demand shocks or inventory cycles.
crazy:rail-carload-contraction-signal.public_summary: Weekly total carloads drop 8% or more from prior-year equivalent week, signaling sharp freight demand collapse and economic slowdown. Rail carload data is a leading indicator of industrial production, construction input, and commodity demand.

normal:cross-asset-volatility-breakout.title: Cross-Asset Volatility Breakout
normal:cross-asset-volatility-breakout.thesis: This model exploits volatility breakouts across multiple asset classes to capture trend initiations. By identifying when an asset's realized volatility exceeds its historical average, the strategy signals potential strong directional moves. The approach diversifies risk by applying the same logic across equities, commodities, and bonds, aiming to enter positions early in emerging trends.
data_sources: yfinance (price data for equities, commodities, bonds)
FRED (for bond yields and macroeconomic indicators if needed)
normal:cross-asset-volatility-breakout.signal_logic: Calculate 20-day realized volatility (rolling standard deviation of daily returns) and 60-day average realized volatility. A volatility breakout signal occurs when the 20-day volatility exceeds 1.5 times the 60-day average volatility.
normal:cross-asset-volatility-breakout.entry_exit: Entry: Buy the asset at next open if volatility breakout signal is present and the 5-day momentum (close/close_5days_ago - 1) is positive. Exit: Sell when 20-day volatility falls below 1.2 times the 60-day average volatility or momentum turns negative.
normal:cross-asset-volatility-breakout.position_sizing: Allocate equal dollar amounts to each asset with an active long signal. If multiple signals are active, divide capital equally among them. No leverage used.
normal:cross-asset-volatility-breakout.risks: False volatility spikes can trigger premature entries leading to whipsaws. Low liquidity or gaps can cause slippage. The model may underperform in low volatility or mean-reverting markets.
normal:cross-asset-volatility-breakout.implementation_notes: 1) Download daily OHLCV data for universe via yfinance. 2) Calculate daily returns and rolling volatilities (20-day and 60-day). 3) Compute 5-day momentum. 4) Generate signals based on volatility breakout and momentum. 5) Backtest entry and exit rules with daily rebalancing. 6) Track positions and returns accordingly.
normal:cross-asset-volatility-breakout.public_summary: This model exploits volatility breakouts across multiple asset classes to capture trend initiations. By identifying when an asset's realized volatility exceeds its historical average, the strategy signals potential strong directional moves. The approach diversifies risk by applying the same logic across equities, commodities, and bonds, aiming to enter positions early in emerging trends.

normal:seasonal-strength-rotation.title: Seasonal Strength Rotation
normal:seasonal-strength-rotation.thesis: Certain asset classes exhibit persistent seasonal patterns in returns due to economic cycles, tax policies, and investor behavior. By identifying the strongest performing assets over specific calendar months and rotating into them monthly, the strategy aims to capture predictable seasonal trends while avoiding weaker assets. This approach leverages well-documented seasonality effects in equity sectors and commodities to enhance returns.
data_sources: yfinance
normal:seasonal-strength-rotation.signal_logic: Calculate average monthly returns for each asset over the past 10 years. For the current month, select the top 3 assets with the highest historical average returns for that month. Rebalance monthly into these assets.
normal:seasonal-strength-rotation.entry_exit: At the start of each month, sell all current holdings and buy equal-weighted positions in the top 3 seasonal performers identified for that month.
normal:seasonal-strength-rotation.position_sizing: Equal weight allocation across the selected top 3 assets each month, fully invested with no leverage.
normal:seasonal-strength-rotation.risks: Seasonality patterns may break down during structural market changes or black swan events. The model may underperform in trending markets where seasonal effects are overshadowed. Monthly rebalancing incurs transaction costs that can erode returns.
normal:seasonal-strength-rotation.implementation_notes: Download 10+ years of daily price data for all universe assets. Aggregate daily returns into monthly returns. Compute average returns per calendar month for each asset. At each month start, rank assets by historical average return for that month and select top 3. Execute trades to equal weight selected assets.
normal:seasonal-strength-rotation.public_summary: Certain asset classes exhibit persistent seasonal patterns in returns due to economic cycles, tax policies, and investor behavior. By identifying the strongest performing assets over specific calendar months and rotating into them monthly, the strategy aims to capture predictable seasonal trends while avoiding weaker assets. This approach leverages well-documented seasonality effects in equity sectors and commodities to enhance returns.

normal:value-momentum-combo.title: Value-Momentum Combo
normal:value-momentum-combo.thesis: Combining value and momentum factors can exploit market inefficiencies by buying undervalued stocks with strong recent performance. Value identifies stocks trading below their fundamental worth, while momentum captures continuation of price trends. This dual-factor approach aims to enhance returns and reduce drawdowns compared to single-factor strategies.
data_sources: yfinance
FRED
normal:value-momentum-combo.signal_logic: Rank stocks monthly by their trailing 12-month price momentum (excluding the most recent month) and their price-to-book (P/B) ratio. Select the top 30% lowest P/B (value) and top 30% highest momentum stocks. The intersection of these two groups forms the buy list.
normal:value-momentum-combo.entry_exit: Enter positions in stocks that are in both top momentum and value groups at the start of each month. Exit positions if the stock falls out of either top 30% group on the next monthly rebalance.
normal:value-momentum-combo.position_sizing: Equal-weight all positions monthly. If fewer than 10 stocks meet criteria, allocate equally among those selected.
normal:value-momentum-combo.risks: Momentum crashes during sudden reversals can cause sharp losses. Value traps where low P/B stocks continue to underperform. Limited diversification if few stocks qualify. Data lookahead bias if momentum excludes most recent month is not properly implemented.
normal:value-momentum-combo.implementation_notes: 1. Download monthly price data for universe using yfinance. 2. Calculate trailing 12-month returns excluding last month for momentum. 3. Retrieve latest price-to-book ratios from yfinance fundamentals or approximate using balance sheet data. 4. Rank stocks by momentum and P/B each month. 5. Select intersection of top 30% momentum and bottom 30% P/B stocks. 6. Generate monthly buy/sell signals based on membership changes. 7. Backtest with equal weighting and track performance.
normal:value-momentum-combo.public_summary: Combining value and momentum factors can exploit market inefficiencies by buying undervalued stocks with strong recent performance. Value identifies stocks trading below their fundamental worth, while momentum captures continuation of price trends. This dual-factor approach aims to enhance returns and reduce drawdowns compared to single-factor strategies.

value-momentum-combo.title: Value-Momentum Combo
value-momentum-combo.thesis: Combining value and momentum factors can exploit market inefficiencies by buying undervalued stocks with strong recent performance. Value identifies stocks trading below their fundamental worth, while momentum captures continuation of price trends. This dual-factor approach aims to enhance returns and reduce drawdowns compared to single-factor strategies.
data_sources: yfinance
FRED
value-momentum-combo.signal_logic: Rank stocks monthly by their trailing 12-month price momentum (excluding the most recent month) and their price-to-book (P/B) ratio. Select the top 30% lowest P/B (value) and top 30% highest momentum stocks. The intersection of these two groups forms the buy list.
value-momentum-combo.entry_exit: Enter positions in stocks that are in both top momentum and value groups at the start of each month. Exit positions if the stock falls out of either top 30% group on the next monthly rebalance.
value-momentum-combo.position_sizing: Equal-weight all positions monthly. If fewer than 10 stocks meet criteria, allocate equally among those selected.
value-momentum-combo.risks: Momentum crashes during sudden reversals can cause sharp losses. Value traps where low P/B stocks continue to underperform. Limited diversification if few stocks qualify. Data lookahead bias if momentum excludes most recent month is not properly implemented.
value-momentum-combo.implementation_notes: 1. Download monthly price data for universe using yfinance. 2. Calculate trailing 12-month returns excluding last month for momentum. 3. Retrieve latest price-to-book ratios from yfinance fundamentals or approximate using balance sheet data. 4. Rank stocks by momentum and P/B each month. 5. Select intersection of top 30% momentum and bottom 30% P/B stocks. 6. Generate monthly buy/sell signals based on membership changes. 7. Backtest with equal weighting and track performance.
